ddls:
CREATE TABLE "bus_routes" (
  "route_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the bus route */,
  "route_number" TEXT /* Number assigned to the bus route */,
  "route_name" TEXT /* Name of the bus route */,
  "start_location" TEXT /* Starting location of the route */,
  "end_location" TEXT /* Ending location of the route */,
  "route_length" REAL /* Distance of the route in kilometers */,
  "estimated_travel_time" INTEGER /* Estimated time to complete the route in minutes */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the route */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("route_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "rider_statistics" (
  "stat_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the statistic record */,
  "route_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the bus route */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date of the statistic record */,
  "day_of_week" INTEGER /* Day of the week (1=Monday, 7=Sunday) */,
  "time_of_day" INTEGER /* Time of day (24-hour format) */,
  "weather_id" INTEGER /* Reference to weather conditions */,
  "number_of_riders" INTEGER /* Number of riders using the bus route at the specified time */,
  "stop_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the bus stop where the statistic was recorded */,
  "is_holiday" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating if the day is a holiday */,
  "average_wait_time" INTEGER /* Average wait time for the bus at the stop in minutes */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("stat_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_rider_statistics_stop_id FOREIGN KEY ("stop_id") REFERENCES bus_stops ("stop_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_rider_statistics_weather_id FOREIGN KEY ("weather_id") REFERENCES weather_conditions ("weather_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_rider_statistics_route_id FOREIGN KEY ("route_id") REFERENCES bus_routes ("route_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "weather_conditions" (
  "weather_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the weather condition */,
  "description" TEXT /* Descriptive name of the weather condition (e.g., Clear, Rainy) */,
  "temperature" REAL /* Average temperature in degrees Celsius */,
  "humidity" INTEGER /* Average humidity percentage */,
  "wind_speed" REAL /* Average wind speed in kilometers per hour */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("weather_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "bus_stops" (
  "stop_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the bus stop */,
  "route_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the bus route it belongs to */,
  "stop_name" TEXT /* Name of the bus stop */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location details of the bus stop */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Latitude of the bus stop */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Longitude of the bus stop */,
  "is_intermodal" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating if the stop is an intermodal station */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("stop_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_bus_stops_route_id FOREIGN KEY ("route_id") REFERENCES bus_routes ("route_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "bus_drivers" (
  "driver_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the bus driver */,
  "name" TEXT /* Full name of the bus driver */,
  "license_number" TEXT /* License number of the driver */,
  "contact_info" TEXT /* Contact information for the driver */,
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the employee in the HR system */,
  "hire_date" TEXT /* Date the driver was hired */,
  "last_training_date" TEXT /* Date of the last training session */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("driver_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "schedules" (
  "schedule_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the schedule entry */,
  "route_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the bus route */,
  "day_of_week" INTEGER /* Day of the week for which the schedule is applicable */,
  "start_time" TEXT /* Start time for the bus route */,
  "end_time" TEXT /* End time for the bus route */,
  "frequency" INTEGER /* Frequency of the bus route in minutes */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the schedule */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("schedule_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "route_assignments" (
  "assignment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the assignment */,
  "route_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the bus route */,
  "driver_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the driver assigned to the route */,
  "schedule_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the schedule for the route */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Date the assignment started */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* Date the assignment ended */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("assignment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_route_assignments_driver_id FOREIGN KEY ("driver_id") REFERENCES bus_drivers ("driver_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_route_assignments_route_id FOREIGN KEY ("route_id") REFERENCES bus_routes ("route_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_route_assignments_schedule_id FOREIGN KEY ("schedule_id") REFERENCES schedules ("schedule_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "maintenance_records" (
  "maintenance_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the maintenance record */,
  "bus_id" INTEGER /* Identifier for the bus undergoing maintenance */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date of the maintenance */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the maintenance performed */,
  "cost" REAL /* Cost incurred for the maintenance */,
  "next_maintenance_date" TEXT /* Date of the next scheduled maintenance */,
  "mechanic_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the mechanic who performed the maintenance */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("maintenance_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_maintenance_records_mechanic_id FOREIGN KEY ("mechanic_id") REFERENCES bus_drivers ("driver_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_maintenance_records_bus_id FOREIGN KEY ("bus_id") REFERENCES buses ("bus_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "buses" (
  "bus_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the bus */,
  "bus_number" TEXT /* Number assigned to the bus */,
  "model" TEXT /* Model of the bus */,
  "capacity" INTEGER /* Seating capacity of the bus */,
  "last_maintenance_date" TEXT /* Date of the last maintenance */,
  "next_maintenance_date" TEXT /* Date of the next scheduled maintenance */,
  "is_operational" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating if the bus is currently operational */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("bus_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "notifications" (
  "notification_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the notification */,
  "message" TEXT /* Message content of the notification */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the notification was created */,
  "is_read" INTEGER /* Indicator if the notification has been read */,
  "bus_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the bus related to the notification */,
  "stop_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the bus stop related to the notification */,
  "route_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the bus route related to the notification */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("notification_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_notifications_bus_id FOREIGN KEY ("bus_id") REFERENCES buses ("bus_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_notifications_stop_id FOREIGN KEY ("stop_id") REFERENCES bus_stops ("stop_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_notifications_route_id FOREIGN KEY ("route_id") REFERENCES bus_routes ("route_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the access log entry */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Identifier for the user accessing the system */,
  "access_time" TEXT /* Timestamp when the access occurred */,
  "action" TEXT /* Description of the action performed */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the user accessing the system */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the device used to access the system */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES bus_drivers ("driver_id")
)
